outcome.headline: Agent fit the correct ordered-logit model but reported the OR for expect_interact='No' (1.318136), the exact reciprocal of the expected OR for 'Yes' (0.759, inside the accepted [0.74,0.77] range).
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Hard-coded numeric range parser: the single number parsed from <answer> must satisfy 0.74 <= value <= 0.77 inclusive (else LLM-judge ensemble); reward 1.0 if in range, 0.0 otherwise.
outcome.what_agent_produced: <answer>1.318136</answer> — the odds ratio for the 'No' indicator of expect_interact_cat (Yes as reference); 1/1.318136 = 0.7586 would have passed.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: The model ran successfully. The odds ratio for expect_interact_cat (expect_No vs Yes) is 1.318136.

failure_modes.name: Inverted odds ratio from wrong reference-category coding
failure_modes.description: C2.1 logical error: agent coded expect_interact with 'No' as the indicator (reference=Yes), so the reported OR is the No-vs-Yes contrast (1.318136) rather than the OR associated with expected interaction (Yes), which is its reciprocal 0.759 and the value the gate accepts
failure_modes.evidence_quote:
X head:
   expect_No  patients_51_100  patients_gt100  trt_BCG

counterfactual.X: agent codes expect_interact with Yes as the indicator (or reports 1/OR = 0.759) to match the question's contrast
counterfactual.Y: answer.txt contains 0.759, the parser finds 0.74<=0.759<=0.77 and awards reward 1.0

failure_modes.name: Reversed expected-interaction contrast
failure_modes.description: The agent encoded and reported the odds ratio for not expecting patient interaction versus expecting interaction. The task's accepted answer corresponds to the inverse contrast, expecting interaction versus not expecting interaction.

counterfactual.X: At result extraction, report the reciprocal of the `expect_No` odds ratio or code `expect_Yes` as the model contrast.
counterfactual.Y: The submitted value would be about 0.7586, inside the verifier's accepted 0.74 to 0.77 range.

failure_modes.name: Reciprocal odds ratio from wrong reference-category direction
failure_modes.description: The question asks for the OR associated with expected patient interaction (expect_interact='Yes'). The agent coded the indicator as expect_No (Yes as reference) and reported the OR for 'No' (1.318136), the multiplicative inverse of the intended OR (0.759). The model fit was statistically correct; only the reported direction was inverted.

counterfactual.X: Code the predictor as expect_interact='Yes' (No as reference), or report the reciprocal 1/1.318136 = 0.759
counterfactual.Y: Submitted value 0.759 falls within [0.74, 0.77] inclusive, so the hard-coded range parser returns reward 1.0

failure_modes.name: No validation of result direction before delivering
failure_modes.description: C7.1 validation missing: at steps 43/46/49/52 the agent only re-verified the answer file format and XML wrapping, never sanity-checking whether the OR's direction matched the question's contrast or the expected protective (sub-1) effect
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The analysis is complete. Let me verify the answer format is correct by wrapping it in XML tags as requested.

failure_modes.name: Incorrect answer-format verification
failure_modes.description: The agent noticed the XML-tag requirement but only echoed a tagged string to stdout, then re-read the file and accepted a file that still lacked <answer> tags. This did not determine the numeric reward because the verifier falls back to raw text, but it violated the explicit submission format.

failure_modes.name: Final answer file was not XML-wrapped
failure_modes.description: After noticing the XML requirement, the agent only echoed a wrapped answer to stdout and then left answer.txt containing the raw number. This did not cause the numeric failure because the verifier accepts raw single numbers, but it violated the task's explicit submission format.

failure_modes.name: All-AE max severity outcome
failure_modes.description: The agent aggregated AESEV as the maximum across all adverse-event rows per subject without filtering to COVID-19 events, despite the question specifying COVID-19 severity.

failure_modes.name: Surface-only verification that ignored a self-raised interpretation doubt
failure_modes.description: The agent explicitly flagged that it should re-check its interpretation of the question, then only re-ran the same code and confirmed the answer-file format/XML wrapping. It never sanity-checked whether the OR direction matched 'expected patient interaction' or whether the value should be <1, shipping the inverted result.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I should also double-check that I'm interpreting the question correctly

failure_modes.name: False XML-format verification
failure_modes.description: After reading that answer.txt contained only a raw number, the agent echoed an XML-wrapped version to stdout but did not write it back to the file, then concluded the file was correctly formatted.

failure_modes.name: Answer-file format not actually fixed
failure_modes.description: After noticing the XML tag requirement, the agent only echoed a wrapped value to stdout and then re-read the still-bare answer file. This violated the explicit submission format, although the numeric value was already outside the accepted range.

counterfactual.X: Write the wrapped value back to /workspace/answer.txt instead of only echoing it.
counterfactual.Y: The final answer file would satisfy the XML wrapper requirement, but the numeric answer would still fail the verifier range.

reward_hacking.evidence: The agent loaded the real data, merged AE max-severity with demographics, fit a genuine OrderedModel, and reported the model's actual coefficient; no shortcutting, no answer fabrication, no targeting of the verifier.

issues: The reference-category convention for the binary predictor is implicit: an OR of 0.759 (Yes vs No) and its reciprocal 1.318 (No vs Yes) describe the same association, but only the 0.74-0.77 form is accepted, penalizing a mathematically equivalent direction the instruction does not disambiguate.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: The hard-coded parser extracts a single number and checks a fixed numeric range; it cannot be gamed other than by submitting a value in range, which requires the actual analysis. The LLM-judge fallback is only reached if no single number parses.

notes_for_aggregation: Near-miss: correct ordered-logit pipeline (n=791, max AESEV per subject) yielded the exact reciprocal of the expected OR because the agent modeled expect_interact='No' instead of 'Yes'. A single reference-direction flip (or reporting 1/value) passes. Secondary failure is verification that confirmed file format but ignored the agent's own flagged doubt about question interpretation.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
